prompt: Use `prochatgpt_researcher` for a focused nephrology/pharmacology audit. This is for a Spanish patient-information site about lipedema. Research, source-first, whether diuretics are merely ineffective for fat-dominant lipedema or truly contraindicated, and map exceptions created by coexisting heart-failure congestion, CKD/nephrotic edema, cirrhosis/ascites, chronic venous disease, medication edema, and lipo-lymphedema. Compare loop diuretics, thiazide/thiazide-like drugs, spironolactone/eplerenone, amiloride/triamterene, acetazolamide, pamabrom, and herbal “water pills.” Explain RAAS/sodium avidity and class-specific electrolyte/acid-base effects, explicitly contrasting loop/thiazide hypochloremic alkalosis with acetazolamide-related bicarbonate loss/acidosis. Prioritize 2021 US and 2024 German lipedema guidance, 2023 ISL consensus, official Spain/EU labels (AEMPS CIMA/EMA), and primary systemic-edema guidelines. Open underlying sources; snippets are not evidence. Extract exact wording, dates, DOI/PMID/URLs, design/sample where relevant, monitoring, contraindications, interactions, limitations and conflicts. Filter clinics/affiliate/product marketing. Perform a falsation pass against both “never use if a person has lipedema” and “all swelling should respond.” Give a phenotype × class matrix and conservative website-safe Spanish wording. Do not give individualized start/stop/taper instructions. Separate direct lipedema evidence, adjacent evidence, mechanism, clinician opinion and anecdote; state unresolved gaps.
